Booleans: BOOLEAN: BER: as 1 byte in binary form;PER: as 1 bit;OER: as 1 byte
Integer: INTEGER: BER: variable-length big-endian binary representation (up to 221024 bits);PER Unaligned: a fixed number of bits if the integer type has a finite range; a variable number of bits otherwise;PER Aligned: a fixed number of bits if the integer type has a finite range and the size of the range is less than 65536; a variable number of octets otherwise;OER: 1, 2, or 4 octets (either signed or unsigned) if the integer type has a finite range that fits in that number of octets; a variable number of octets otherwise
Floating-point: REAL:base-10 real values are represented as character strings in ISO 6093 format;binary real values are represented in a binary format that includes the mantissa, the base (2, 8, or 16), and the exponent;the special values NaN, -INF, +INF, and negative zero are also supported
String: Multiple valid types (VisibleString, PrintableString, GeneralString, UniversalString, UTF8String)
Array: Data specifications SET OF (unordered) and SEQUENCE OF (guaranteed order)

Integer: int32: 32-bit little-endian 2's complement or int64: 64-bit little-endian 2's complement
String: UTF-8-encoded, preceded by int32-encoded string length in bytes
Array: BSON embedded document with numeric keys

Integer: Small positive/negative \x00–\x17 & \x20–\x37 (1 byte)8-bit: positive \x18, negative \x38 (+ 1 byte)16-bit: positive \x19, negative \x39 (+ 2 bytes)32-bit: positive \x1A, negative \x3A (+ 4 bytes)64-bit: positive \x1B, negative \x3B (+ 8 bytes)Negative x encoded as (−x − 1)
Floating-point: IEEE half/single/double \xf9–\xfb (+ 2–8 bytes)Decimals and bigfloats (4+ bytes) encoded as \xc4 tag + 2-item array of integer mantissa & exponent
String: Length and content (1–9 bytes overhead)Bytestring \x40–\x5fUTF-8 \x60–\x7fIndefinite partial strings \x5f and \x7f stitched together until \xff.
Array: Length and items \x80–\x9eIndefinite list \x9f terminated by \xff entry.
Associative array/object: Length (in pairs) and items \xa0–\xbeIndefinite map \xbf terminated by \xff key.

Format:
Efficient XML Interchange (EXI)

(Unpreserved lexical values format)
Null: xsi:nil is not allowed in binary context.
Integer:
Boolean sign, plus arbitrary length 7-bit octets, parsed until most-significant bit is 0, in little-endian. The schema can set the zero-point to any arbitrary number.

Unsigned skips the boolean flag.
Floating-point: Float: integer mantissa and integer exponent.Decimal: boolean sign, integer whole value, integer fractional.
String: Length prefixed integer-encoded Unicode. Integers may represent enumerations or string table entries instead.

Null: Encoded as absence of field in parent object
Integer: Little-endian 2's complement signed and unsigned 8/16/32/64 bits
String: UTF-8-encoded, preceded by 32-bit integer length of string in bytes
Array: Vectors of any other type, preceded by 32-bit integer length of number of elements
Associative array/object: Tables (schema defined types) or Vectors sorted by key (maps / dictionaries)

Integer: Positive \x2x, negative \x3xZero is always encoded in tag byte.BigInts over 13 bytes (104 bits) have 1+ byte overhead for length
Floating-point: \x44 (32-bit float)\x48 (64-bit float)Zero is always encoded in tag byte.
Array: \xbx Arbitrary length and overhead. Length in octets.

String:
Typecode + up to 15 bytesor typecode + length as uint8/16/32 + bytes;
encoding is unspecified
Array: As "fixarray" (single-byte prefix + up to 15 array items)or typecode (1 byte) + 2–4 bytes length + array items
Associative array/object: As "fixmap" (single-byte prefix + up to 15 key-value pairs)or typecode (1 byte) + 2–4 bytes length + key-value pairs

String:
Length-encoded as an ASCII string + ':' + data + ','

Length counts only octets between ':' and ','

Integer: Variable encoding length signed 32-bit: varint encoding of "ZigZag"-encoded value (n << 1) XOR (n >> 31)Variable encoding length signed 64-bit: varint encoding of "ZigZag"-encoded (n << 1) XOR (n >> 63)Constant encoding length 32-bit: 32 bits in little-endian 2's complementConstant encoding length 64-bit: 64 bits in little-endian 2's complement
String: UTF-8-encoded, preceded by varint-encoded integer length of string in bytes
Array: Repeated value with the same tag or, for varint-encoded integers only, values packed contiguously and prefixed by tag and total byte length

Integer: Single byte "small" (values −16 – 15 encoded as \xc0–\xdf),zigzag-encoded varints (1–11 data bytes), or BigInteger
String: Length-prefixed "short" Strings (up to 64 bytes), marker-terminated "long" Strings and (optional) back-references

Array: List of elements with identical ID and size, preceded by array header with int16 length
Associative array/object: Chunks can contain other chunks to arbitrary depth.
